headlines.title: Xcel Energy (XEL) Stock May Trade At A Dividend Premium But Fairly On Earnings
headlines.summary: Xcel Energy has delivered a solid 42.3% return over the past three years, yet the latest valuation checks suggest the stock may be pricing in a full outlook, with the Dividend Discount Model (DDM) pointing to a premium to its intrinsic value and market multiples sitting closer to fair. Over the past three years, Xcel Energy shareholders have seen a 42.3% total return, which puts recent gains front and center in any valuation view.

headlines.title: Xcel Energy (XEL) Reports Next Week: Wall Street Expects Earnings Growth
headlines.summary: Xcel (XEL) doesn't possess the right combination of the two key ingredients for a likely earnings beat in its upcoming report. Get prepared with the key expectations.

headlines.title: Xcel Energy (XEL) Could Be 12% Undervalued After Clean Energy And AI Demand Focus
headlines.summary: Recent commentary around Xcel Energy (XEL) has sharpened attention on how the utility is positioned at the intersection of clean power development, expanding data center and AI infrastructure needs, and evolving regulatory and balance sheet pressures. See our latest analysis for Xcel Energy. At a share price of US$80.19, Xcel Energy has seen steady positive momentum, with a 7.38% year to date share price return and a 5 year total shareholder return of 36.90%.

headlines.title: From Chip to Grid to Orbit: Yotta 2026  Launches Full Agenda
headlines.summary: The complete program for the Sept. 28-30 Las Vegas event is now live, with 250+ speakers spanning every corner of the AI infrastructure ecosystem. Pass prices increase Aug. 21.NEW YORK, July 22, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Yotta 2026 today launched the full agenda for the industry’s largest event at the intersection of AI, energy and digital infrastructure.

description: Xcel Energy Inc., through its subsidiaries, operates as an electric and natural gas delivery company in the United States. It operates through Regulated Electric Utility and Regulated Natural Gas Utility segments. The company generates, purchases, transmits, distributes, and sells electricity through its energy portfolio, including wind, nuclear, hydroelectric, biomass, and solar power from both owned generation facilities and PPAs, as well as its fossil fuel energy portfolio, such as coal and natural gas; sale and resale of wholesale transmission service; and engages in wholesale commodity and trading operations. It also purchases, transports, stores, distributes, and sells natural gas; develops and leases natural gas pipelines and storage facilities; operates interstate natural gas pipeline; and invests in rental housing projects, energy technology companies, and community solar garden nonregulated assets. It serves electric and natural gas customers in portions of Colorado, Michigan, Minnesota, New Mexico, North Dakota, South Dakota, Texas, and Wisconsin. The company was formerly known as Northern States Power company. Xcel Energy Inc. was incorporated in 1909 and is headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota.
